data.title: Fed Admits Conducting 'Extremely Rare' Dollar-Yen 'Rate Check' On White House Request
data.description:
The Federal Reserve on Wednesday acknowledged performing a “rate check” on the dollar-yen exchange rate for the White House, hinting at possible active intervention in the currency markets.

The Fed's January meeting minutes show that private market participants had expected the dollar to continue weakening this year. However, stronger-than-anticipated performance in the U.S. economy has cooled those projections. Meanwhile, the dollar has been steadily appreciating against the yen, moving closer to the ¥160 level

Subsequently, the U.S. Treasury asked the Fed’s trading desk to quote a large yen purchase, a strategy that would depreciate the dollar and boost the Japanese currency.

“The dollar had depreciated markedly after reports that the Desk had made requests for indicative quotes, known as ‘rate checks,’ on the dollar–yen exchange rate,” stated the minutes.

On January 23, the dollar was trading at ¥158.50 but slid sharply to ¥152.45 by January 27, marking an unusually steep move for major global currencies.

The manager clarified that the Desk sought the quotes “solely” on behalf of the U.S. Treasury, acting in its capacity as the New York Fed's fiscal agent.

‘Extremely Rare’ Move By White House

The “rate check” move suggests the White House prefers a weaker dollar to make U.S. goods and services more affordable for foreign buyers, thereby supporting exports and attracting more foreign investment into the country.

Dollar Slides, Trump Unfazed

Disclaimer: This content was partially produced with the help of AI tools and was reviewed and published by Benzinga editors.

data.title: What's Going On With Tiny AI-Tech Company Gaxos.ai On Thursday?
data.description:
Gaxos.ai (NASDAQ:GXAI) shares are up during Thursday’s premarket session. However, there is no news to justify the movement.

On Tuesday, the company announced a partnership with Byteplus, the Enterprise Technology Division of ByteDance. This collaboration is expected to enhance Gaxos’ AI capabilities significantly, adding pressure as broader markets edged lower.

Byteplus Partnership Boosts Gaxos.ai’s AI Capabilities

Under the agreement announced on Tuesday, Gaxos will receive preferred pricing and discounts on ByteDance’s advanced video generation models, which will accelerate the deployment of AI tools in its flagship platform, Art-Gen.

This strategic alignment is anticipated to enhance Gaxos’ gross margins and market penetration in the rapidly growing creator economy.

Additionally, the partnership will provide Gaxos with early access to new technologies, further solidifying its competitive edge in the AI space. The CEO, Vadim Mats, emphasized that this collaboration is a significant step toward establishing Gaxos as a leading AI platform for creators and enterprises.

Gaxos.ai’s Mixed Short-Term Trading Trends

Gaxos.ai is currently trading 3.9% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 8.1% above its 50-day SMA, reflecting a mixed short-term trend. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 23.60%, and they are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 48.78, which is considered neutral territory. Meanwhile, MACD is at -0.0078, below its signal line at 0.0201, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $1.50
  • Key Support: $1.00

GXAI Price Action: Gaxos.AI shares were up 3.25% at $1.27 during premarket trading on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: These Analysts Cut Their Forecasts On Amplitude After Q4 Results
data.description:
Amplitude Inc (NASDAQ:AMPL) reported in-line earnings for the fourth quarter on Wednesday.

The company posted quarterly earnings of 4 cents per share which met the analyst consensus estimate. The company reported quarterly sales of $91.427 million which beat the analyst consensus estimate of $90.344 million.

Amplitude said it sees FY2026 adjusted EPS of 8 cents-13 cents versus market estimates of 12 cents. The company sees sales of $390.000 million-$398.000 million, versus estimates of $390.120 million.

“We’re entering a new era of analytics—one where AI can monitor your product around the clock, and free up your team to focus on improving the experience,” said Spenser Skates, co-founder and CEO of Amplitude. “The real advantage is how quickly a team can learn, iterate, improve, and automate. Agentic analytics is the key.”

Amplitude shares closed at $7.18 on Wednesday.

These analysts made changes to their price targets on Amplitude following earnings announcement.

  • Piper Sandler analyst Billy Fitzsimmons maintained Amplitude with an Overweight rating and lowered the price target from $12 to $10.
  • BTIG analyst Nick Altmann maintained the stock with a Buy and lowered the price target from $14 to $10.

Considering buying AMPL stock? Here’s what analysts think:
